briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby North Road BIA Renewal — Five-Year Funding and Local Tax Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would renew the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for five years starting April 1, 2026, and authorize over $1.75 million in grants to the local business association — funded through a special tax on commercial and light-industrial property owners within the BIA boundary.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property falls within the Burnaby North Road BIA boundary — you may owe a new local tax.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw — proposed five-year term takes effect
Burnaby North Road BIA — annual budget submission deadline to City
Burnaby North Road BIA — annual grant payment deadline from City
Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw — proposed five-year term ends

briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Proposed: Amenity Pier Covenant for 8351 Fraser Reach Court, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a covenant — a legal agreement registered on a property title — related to an amenity pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court in Burnaby's Riverbend Business Park area near the Fraser River. The sketches show the subject site's location and its relationship to the Big Bend Community Plan, including a previously proposed pier location and nearby public trail connections.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near Fraser Reach Court, monitor this proposal for impacts on site access or development plans.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Amenity Pier Covenant — Council consideration meeting date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Developer Forfeits $1.36M Instead of Building Fraser River Pier
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive information about Oxford Properties' decision to pay $1,363,500 to the City of Burnaby rather than build a planned public pier on the Fraser River. The money will be held by the City and may be used for a future public amenity project.
briefs.action_required: Watch for how Council directs the $1.36M — it may fund future amenities near your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Amenity Pier forfeiture report received for information

briefs.title: Council to consider update on Burnaby Task Force shaping local economic growth strategy
briefs.summary: Council will consider a progress report on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience, which is developing recommendations to strengthen local businesses and the broader economy. The Task Force is expected to present its final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Task Force's final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026 — they could shape city support for your business.

key_dates.label: Mayor's Task Force final recommendations presented to Burnaby Council

briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning Prerequisites Set for 5035 Beresford Street Development, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a list of conditions a developer must meet before a rezoning bylaw for 5035 Beresford Street can be formally adopted. These prerequisites include engineering upgrades, road dedications, legal agreements, and housing requirements.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 5035 Beresford Street, watch for road and sidewalk construction on Beresford Street and Dunblane Avenue.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ 25-11 rezoning prerequisites council consideration date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: 20-Storey Rental Apartment at 5035 Beresford Street Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider rezoning 5035 Beresford Street to allow a 20-storey, 215-unit market rental apartment building. The proposed development would include a mix of studio, one-, two-, and three-bedroom units, with all parking stalls built EV-ready.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Burnaby Council meeting for the rezoning vote — this affects nearby property owners and the local rental market.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rezoning REZ 25-11 — Council to Consider Building Elevation Plans
briefs.summary: Council is set to consider architectural elevation drawings (the exterior design plans showing what a building looks like from each side) submitted as part of rezoning application REZ 25-11 in Burnaby. These drawings form part of a larger rezoning proposal that has not yet been approved.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council meeting agendas for the REZ 25-11 vote if this development affects your property or business.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider Rezoning with Shadow Study and Site Plan — REZ 25-11
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to review a rezoning application (REZ 25-11) that includes a shadow study, site plan, and subdivision plan for a development project. No decision has been made yet — these documents are part of the materials Council will consider before voting.
briefs.action_required: Check the Burnaby Council meeting agenda for REZ 25-11 to see if this affects your property or business.

briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning of 5035 Beresford Street, Burnaby — Council to Consider
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a rezoning application (REZ 25-11) for the property at 5035 Beresford Street. The proposal would change the land-use rules — meaning what can be built or operated there — as part of the Royal Oak Community Plan area.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business or property is near 5035 Beresford St and attend the public hearing to have your say.

key_dates.label: REZ 25-11 Burnaby rezoning application — agenda date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Real Estate

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider Rezoning 5035 Beresford Street for 20-Storey Rental Tower
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a rezoning application that would allow a 20-storey, 215-unit rental apartment building to replace a printing shop at 5035 Beresford Street in the Royal Oak area. The proposal must clear several more steps — including First and Second Reading of a rezoning bylaw — before it can be finally approved.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council meeting where the rezoning bylaw gets its First and Second Reading — this affects nearby properties.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #25-11 rezoning bylaw — Planning & Development Committee consideration

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on hiring an external reviewer to assess Burnaby's financial practices
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on hiring an outside expert to review how the City plans its budget, manages day-to-day spending, and prioritizes major investments — with the goal of keeping tax increases manageable for businesses and residents.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — findings could influence future tax and service cost changes.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — external financial review approval

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Professional Services, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Commemorative Plaque for Burnaby Lake Overpass Opening
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the wording for a commemorative plaque marking the official opening of the Burnaby Lake Overpass — a new pedestrian and cyclist bridge over Highway 1 — planned for April 26, 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Lake Overpass official opening — pedestrian/cyclist bridge opens

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby Lake Overpass commemorative plaque wording
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the exact wording for a commemorative plaque to be installed at the new Burnaby Lake Overpass, a pedestrian and cyclist bridge over Highway 1. The grand opening is scheduled for April 26, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14 council meeting if you have an interest in the Burnaby Lake Overpass opening.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Lake Overpass grand opening event

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Renewing fire protection services contract with A.W Fireguard
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with A.W Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. to inspect, maintain, and repair fire protection and emergency lighting systems at City buildings — a three-year deal worth roughly $1.67 million (taxes included), running July 2026 to June 2029.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — this affects fire services at City facilities.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fire protection services contract renewal — proposed start date
Burnaby Council vote on fire protection contract renewal

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Renewing Cisco Software Contracts Worth $442,779
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing two Cisco software contracts — one for call centre software (UCCX) and one for video conferencing (WebEx) — totalling roughly $442,779 for the renewal period, an 18.6% increase over the previous term due to higher unit prices.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you supply or compete for City technology contracts.

key_dates.label: Cisco WebEx contract renewal pending Council approval — start date
Cisco WebEx contract renewal term ends

briefs.title: Council to vote on $394,942 Cisco WebEx Contact Center license renewal for Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on renewing a three-year software contract with Long View Systems Corporation to keep Burnaby's phone-based customer service system running from May 2026 to May 2029.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Cisco WebEx Contact Center contract renewal period begins

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.04M increase to Burnaby's HVAC maintenance contract
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a $1,043,700 increase to an existing contract with Just Mechanical Ltd. for heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) upkeep at City-owned buildings. The increase covers added services at four new sites, bringing the total contract value to roughly $4.83 million.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects public facility maintenance spending in Burnaby.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby HVAC Service Contract Renewal and Additional Funding
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing an HVAC (heating, ventilation, and air conditioning) maintenance contract and approving an additional $579,600, bringing the total estimated contract value to $5,412,750 since 2021.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the council decision if you supply HVAC services to the City of Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby HVAC contract renewal #2 term begins
Burnaby HVAC contract renewal #2 term ends

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract extension for Burnaby city building HVAC services
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on extending its heating and cooling (HVAC) maintenance contract with Just Mechanical Ltd. for an estimated $579,600 including taxes, while the City runs a new competitive bidding process for a long-term replacement contract.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if you provide HVAC or facilities services to the City.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — HVAC contract change vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on two-year Rogers cellular contract extension worth $1.1 million
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on extending its cellular phone and data services contract with Rogers Communications for two more years, at an estimated cost of $1,135,949 (including taxes). The extension would cover staff mobile phones, iPads, and Wi-Fi hotspots at Burnaby Public Library from October 2023 to October 2025.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you supply telecom or mobile services to the City of Burnaby.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Telephony Contract Extension Worth Up to $450,000
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed month-to-month contract change that would extend the City's telephone services agreement for up to 9 months at a cost of $450,000, bringing the total estimated contract value to roughly $3.5 million since 2020.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the council decision if you supply telecom or IT services to the City of Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby telephony contract extension (pending approval) start date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450,000 contract extension with Rogers for city cellular services
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on extending its cellular voice and data contract with Rogers Communications by $450,000 (including taxes) while the city transitions to a new provider selected through a public bidding process.
briefs.action_required: No action required for most businesses — this is an internal city contract decision.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M contract for Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving work
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. to replace 16 culverts and complete final paving along the Central Valley Greenway trail between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14 council decision if your business operates near the Central Valley Greenway trail.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — contract award vote for Central Valley Greenway works

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M contract for Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. to replace an undersized culvert at Jerry Rogers Creek with a larger box culvert built to handle major flood events.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near Jerry Rogers Creek or Marine Drive.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Jerry Rogers Creek culvert contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M contract for modular four-plex housing pilot in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular four-unit rental home at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project testing factory-built, low-carbon housing construction.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — it could signal more modular housing projects in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — modular four-plex contract award decision

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider Statutory Rights-of-Way at Two City-Owned Sites
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on granting three Statutory Rights-of-Way (SRWs) — legal agreements that allow specific use of portions of city-owned land — at 7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way near Simon Fraser University.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Burnaby Council meeting agenda for the vote date if your business operates near these two sites.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on granting FortisBC a right-of-way on Burnaby Mountain land
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on allowing FortisBC Energy Inc. to access portions of two city-owned properties on Burnaby Mountain — a legal access agreement called a Statutory Right-of-Way — so FortisBC can install and maintain natural gas lines serving the Mintara restaurant and nearby park facilities.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed for most businesses — monitor the April 14, 2026 council decision if you operate near Burnaby Mountain.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — FortisBC Burnaby Mountain right-of-way approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's Role in Push for Mandatory BC Building Energy Reporting
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider the Building Benchmark BC Year 5 Annual Report, which calls on the BC provincial government to make energy benchmarking — tracking and publicly reporting how much energy a building uses — mandatory for large commercial and multi-family buildings starting in 2026.
briefs.action_required: If you own or manage a large commercial or multi-family building, prepare to track your building's energy use now.

key_dates.label: BC mandatory building energy benchmarking proposed start year — commercial and multi-family buildings

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby joining Building Benchmark BC energy tracking program for Year 7
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on continuing the city's partnership with Building Benchmark BC, a voluntary program that helps building owners track and compare their energy use and carbon emissions. No extra budget is needed — the estimated $16,500 cost would come from the Planning and Development Department's existing 2026 budget.
briefs.action_required: If you own a building in Burnaby, consider signing up for Building Benchmark BC to get free energy and emissions data.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Building Benchmark BC Year 7 council vote — public meeting

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's First Housing Target Progress Report to BC Province
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving and submitting its first Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing. The report covers September 2025 to February 2026 and shows 910 net new homes were completed, with tens of thousands more in the approval pipeline.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's April 14 vote — new zoning and development rules in this report may affect your property or projects.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Target Progress Report — Council resolution vote
Burnaby Development Permit system (Form & Character, Streamside, Tenant Protection) takes effect
Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw — expected Council consideration

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: BC Province Orders Burnaby to Build 10,240 New Homes by 2030
briefs.summary: The BC Minister of Housing has issued a Housing Target Order requiring Burnaby to complete at least 10,240 net new housing units between September 1, 2025 and August 31, 2030. Council is set to consider this order, which sets yearly building milestones and requires regular progress reports to the Province.
briefs.action_required: Watch for council's response — faster housing approvals in Burnaby could open new development opportunities.

key_dates.label: BC Provincial Housing Target Order for Burnaby takes effect
Burnaby Housing Target Order — Reporting Period 1.1 ends
Burnaby Housing Target Order — Year 1 progress report due
Burnaby Housing Target Order — Year 2 progress report due
Burnaby Housing Target Order — Year 3 progress report due
Burnaby Housing Target Order — Year 4 progress report due
Burnaby Provincial Housing Target Order ends

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Real Estate & Finance

briefs.title: Council to vote on submitting Burnaby's 6-month housing target progress report to BC
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on authorizing staff to send Burnaby's first progress report to the BC Ministry of Housing, showing 910 new homes were built in the first six months of a five-year, 10,240-unit provincial housing target. Slowing construction and a pause in provincial affordable housing funding are putting future targets at risk.
briefs.action_required: If you work in construction or development, watch this file — slowing approvals and funding cuts could affect your project pipeline.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Year 1b Housing Target Progress Report due to Province

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Bylaw Changes Would Set New Rules for E-Bikes and Electric Scooters
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on bylaw amendments that would create new rules for shared e-bikes and electric kick scooters in Burnaby — covering where they can be parked, where they can be ridden, and what fines apply for breaking the rules.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if you operate near bike-share zones or use e-scooters for deliveries, this could affect you.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Bylaw Changes to Allow E-Bike Share and E-Scooter Programs
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on bylaw amendments that would set rules for a new shared e-bike rental program and privately owned electric kick scooters on Burnaby streets and in parks.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new parking, riding zones, and rules could affect your business or deliveries.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — e-bike share and e-scooter bylaw amendments vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Housing Authority Proposed 2026 Operating Budget
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review the Burnaby Housing Authority's proposed 2026 operating budget. The budget outlines planned spending and revenue for the city's affordable housing programs for the coming year.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect affordable housing availability and costs in Burnaby.

briefs.title: Council to consider: Burnaby Housing Authority's first audited financial statements
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review the first audited financial statements for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA), covering its launch in June 2024 through December 31, 2025. The BHA received $37.5M from the City and is actively developing two affordable housing projects.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this shapes how public housing funds are managed in Burnaby.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to Consider Burnaby Housing Authority's 2025 Financials and 2026 Budget
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive the Burnaby Housing Authority's first audited financial statements and its 2026 operating budget — a required step before the City can release $2 million in funding to support new affordable and market rental housing projects.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it unlocks $2M for new rental housing projects in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Authority 2026 operating funding — council receipt meeting
BHA $25M GIC investment matures — Burnaby Housing Authority

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby Housing Authority annual governance and board appointments for 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on routine annual housekeeping matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) — the City's own housing company — including appointing KPMG as auditor, confirming 10 board directors, and setting December 12, 2026 as the official annual reference date (the stand-in date for the yearly shareholder meeting).
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects Burnaby's public housing development direction and board leadership.

key_dates.label: BHA annual reference date set — Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to Consider Renewing North Road BIA for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a Certificate of Sufficiency for the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a formal confirmation that enough local businesses support renewing the BIA for 2026. If approved, businesses in the North Road area would continue to pay a special levy (an extra fee added to property taxes) that funds local marketing, events, and streetscape improvements.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business is in the North Road BIA zone — this levy could affect your 2026 costs.

key_dates.label: North Road BIA Certificate of Sufficiency — Burnaby Council vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: North Road BIA Renewal Blocked by Sufficient Owner Opposition
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report showing that enough property owners on North Road signed against renewing the Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special tax zone that funds local business programs — to legally block the renewal. Over 55% of affected property owners opposed it, meeting the 50% threshold required to stop the bylaw from passing.
briefs.action_required: If you own or rent on North Road, watch for updates — the BIA renewal has failed and funding stops April 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA grant funding ends — Association loses 2026–2027 funding

briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on appointing new Chief Election Officer for Burnaby's 2026 election
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the October 17, 2026 municipal election, replacing Samantha Pellizzari who is on leave and not expected to return in time.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects who will run Burnaby's 2026 general local election.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026 General Local Election takes place

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Declaring April 28 as Day of Mourning for Workers
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider a request from the New Westminster & District Labour Council to officially declare April 28 as the 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job,' observe a minute of silence at a Council meeting, and hear a 5-minute delegation on workplace health and safety.
briefs.action_required: If workplace safety affects your business, attend the April 14 Council meeting to hear the delegation's update on WorkSafeBC changes.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Day of Mourning delegation presentation
Day of Mourning for Workers — April 28 observance date

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Declaring April 28th Day of Mourning for Injured and Killed Workers
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a request from the New Westminster & District Labour Council to officially recognize April 28th as the 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job,' observe a minute of silence in chambers, and encourage staff to do the same at 11:00 a.m. on April 28th.
briefs.action_required: If worker safety matters to your business, attend the April 14 Council meeting to hear the Labour Council's presentation.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Labour Council delegation presentation
Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job — April 28

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Construction & Development, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Letter Urging Federal Action on Chlorine Rail Transport Safety Risks
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to send a letter to the federal Transport Minister raising serious safety concerns about liquid chlorine being transported by rail through densely populated areas of the Lower Mainland — a risk tied to a chlorine facility in North Vancouver that is currently seeking approval to keep operating past 2030.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near a Burnaby rail corridor, monitor the North Vancouver bylaw vote on April 13, 2026.

key_dates.label: District of North Vancouver — Chemtrade chlorine bylaw amendment third reading scheduled

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Advances Several Rezoning and Housing Proposals at March 24 Meeting
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on a range of proposals including new rezoning bylaws for mixed-use and residential developments, a $4.2M water metering contract, and a modular housing pilot project that was sent back to staff for further review.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property near 6535 Kingsway, 5058 Imperial St, or Carrigan Court, watch for upcoming public hearings on rezoning bylaws.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics
